title: Community Gardens | San Francisco Recreation and Parks, CA
content: # Community Gardens. SFRPD supports and manages 42 (and growing!) Community Gardens located across the City where garden volunteers can grow produce and ornamental plants for personal use. There are several ways to participate in one of our Community Gardens. **Individual plots allotted via a wait-list system:**Some community gardens are composed entirely of plots allotted to individuals with some common areas for shared perennial herbs, native plants and fruit trees. These gardens may have regularly scheduled workdays, self-imposed annual dues to purchase shared garden tools and equipment, and either an individual volunteer garden coordinator or a steering committee that manages membership, workday plans, and plot assignments. Individuals can request to be placed on the wait list for a specific garden. 1. You must be a resident of San Francisco to be allocated a plot; and,. Once a plot is available, the volunteer garden coordinator will contact the next name on the wait list to offer a plot.

title: San Francisco Botanical Garden - All You SHOULD Know ...
content: Things to Do in San Francisco. See all things to do. # San Francisco Botanical Garden. Tripadvisor gives a Travelers’ Choice award to accommodations, attractions and restaurants that consistently earn great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties on Tripadvisor. Welcome to San Francisco Botanical Garden, one of the most diverse gardens in the world. The Botanical Garden is a living museum within Golden Gate Park, offering 55 acres of both landscaped gardens and open spaces, showcasing 8,000 different kinds of plants from around the world. ## San Francisco Botanical Garden and other featured experiences. Find things to do near San Francisco Botanical Garden Sights, experiences, and restaurants picked just for you. *   Image 40      rylUSaOrlando, Florida 104 contributions             A Botanical Escape in the Heart of SF 🌿🌸 The San Francisco Botanical Garden is way more than just a pretty park—it’s like a passport to nature from all over the world.
